title: vriesea friburgensis var. paludosa
intro: Vriesea friburgensis var. paludosa, also known as the swamp Tillandsia, is a type of epiphytic plant belonging to the bromeliad family. Native to Brazil, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Vriesea friburgensis var. paludosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and provide relief from symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the extract as a cough syrup or expectorant.
